top_findings.summary: The skill requests broad Kubernetes cluster access (kubectl), system-level logging (journalctl), and network traffic capture (tcpdump) for debugging Replit issues, which is excessive. While debugging production issues may require some access, the skill as described can read logs from all pods (`-l app=replit-integration` is a label selector that could match many resources), capture all port 443 traffic, and access Kubernetes secrets—far beyond the minimal scope needed to diagnose a specific Replit integration problem. This violates the principle of least privilege and data minimization.
